blocks.content: The block went colder. The days got shorter. Diego still showed up at the park some evenings — sometimes I'd see him on the way home and we'd sit for a few minutes before it got too dark. He never took me up on the dinner offer but he stopped saying I'm good to it. That felt like progress.

Jonathan won his second game of the season. Larry Jr. was there again — said he was going to every one now, like that was a decision he'd made and nobody had signed off on it. Mr. Cole drove us both times without being asked.

Daniel Snyder stayed quiet. The confrontation in the hall had gone nowhere — he'd backed down in front of me twice and there was nobody watching the second time, which meant it was real and not just performance. Something had shifted in the equation. I didn't trust it fully. But it was different.

Maya and I had a real conversation. Not about anything specific. About what we wanted after high school. She wanted to study design somewhere far enough away that nobody already knew her version of herself. I said I understood that. I did.

November is the month where everything that's going to be a thing starts to feel like a thing. I could feel it — the year finding its permanent shape. Some of it good. Some of it still uncertain. All of it mine.

blocks.prompt: November. The year finding its shape. What does that feel like from where Larry is standing?

options.label: Solid. Like the ground actually holds him now.
There's still something unresolved. He can feel it out there.
